what_it_does: Renders a dropdown chip that lets the operator choose between a full-float and a binary (sign-only) representation for vector-space projections. It only appears when the current projection has a binding.space, opens a small animated menu, and calls setQuant with 'binary' or null.
observations: Only renders when proj?.binding?.space is truthy; hidden on structural lenses.
Uses a local OPTS array with ids 'full' and 'binary' and labels 'Full' and 'Coarse'.
Uses the useChipMenu hook for open/toggle/close state, a wrapper ref, and menu class.
Uses framer-motion AnimatePresence and motion.ul for enter/exit animation.
Applies stamp attributes to the chip root, current button, and each option button.
The displayed current label is lowercased ('full'/'coarse') while OPTS labels are capitalized.
Selecting 'full' calls setQuant(null); selecting 'binary' calls setQuant('binary').
The title text explicitly tells the user that binary is not the speed lever and points to the detail picker.
Large inline comments explain the geometry, compute-on-read purity, registry truth, and an honest performance note.
standouts: Extensive inline commentary that documents design rationale, operator-law concerns, and an explicit performance disclaimer.
The title actively redirects speed expectations to the detail picker instead of the representation picker.
State encoding is asymmetric: 'full' is represented by null, 'binary' by the string 'binary'.
Uses stamp() for semantic UI addressing/telemetry on multiple elements.
conventions: React functional component with explicit props type.
Local constant array for selectable options.
Conditional early return for inapplicable projection types.
Dropdown with scrim overlay to close on outside click.
aria-expanded on the toggle button and aria-hidden on the decorative caret.
concerns: Label casing mismatch between OPTS labels ('Full'/'Coarse') and the rendered display text ('full'/'coarse').
Asymmetric state mapping ('full' -> null) may be surprising without seeing the parent state contract.
Comments note the operator-facing labels are 'TENTATIVE — AI-drafted', suggesting they may need human review.
menuClass is interpolated into a className string without additional safety checks visible in this file.
notes: A vector-lens-only representation toggle that is unusually explicit about what it does not do (speed optimization).
questions: How does the parent interpret a null quant versus 'binary'?
What does useChipMenu return exactly (menuClass semantics, positioning logic)?
Is the backend prepared to serve binary-only geometry on demand?
What is the stamp() signature and where are the stamped identifiers consumed?
Have the AI-drafted labels been reviewed/approved since the comment was written?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a representation picker for vector lenses; the component does exactly that, and the inline notes match the implemented behavior.
intention_signals: The file is trying to give operators a clear, non-misleading choice between exact and coarse vector geometry while integrating cleanly into the chip-menu UI pattern.
novelty: The explicit in-code and in-UI disclaimer that binary representation is not a speed win, redirecting users to the detail picker for performance.
contribution: Provides a focused, conditionally rendered UI control for switching vector representation geometry, with built-in semantic addressing and transparent labeling about its actual effect.
summary_for_embedding: QuantChip is a React dropdown chip that toggles between full-float and binary-sign vector representations for vector-space projections, using framer-motion and a chip-menu hook.
intention_for_embedding: Provide an honest, geometry-focused representation picker that avoids conflating binary quantization with performance gains.

symbols.code_id: code://company/surface/app/src/toggles/QuantChip.tsx::QuantChip | component | export function QuantChip({ proj, quant, setQuant }:
symbols.does: Renders an animated dropdown chip for choosing full or binary vector representation.
symbols.description: QuantChip is an exported React component that conditionally appears only for projections with a vector binding space. It manages an open/close menu via useChipMenu, renders a toggle button and animated option list with framer-motion, and calls setQuant with null for 'full' or 'binary' for the coarse sign-based geometry. It also applies stamp() attributes to root, current, and option elements for UI telemetry/addressing.
